our_product.name: NeverMiss
our_product.what_you_get: Intelligent AI text-back within 60 seconds, 24/7/365, unlimited leads, learns your business, books appointments automatically
our_product.hidden_costs: None. Flat rate, no per-minute, no per-call, no setup fees.
key_differentiators: Learns your specific business and services

smith_ai.name: Smith.ai
smith_ai.what_you_get: Live human receptionists answer phone calls during business hours
hidden_costs: Overage charges per call beyond plan limit
Setup fee for complex call flows
weaknesses: Business hours only (M-F 6am-6pm PT typical)
Humans = inconsistent quality across reps
Phone-first, text-back is add-on or unavailable
Per-call pricing means cost spikes with volume
After-hours gap loses emergency calls
No AI learning -- scripts are static
Long hold times during peak periods

ruby.name: Ruby
ruby.what_you_get: Live human receptionists answering phone calls, per-minute billing
hidden_costs: Per-minute billing means chatty callers cost more
No control over call duration = unpredictable bills
Add-on fees for outbound calls, appointment booking
weaknesses: Per-minute billing is unpredictable and expensive
A 5-minute call on the base plan costs $23.50 in overage
Live humans cannot scale instantly
Quality varies by receptionist
Text/chat features are secondary, not core
Monthly minute pool creates anxiety about usage
Business hours focus -- after-hours is limited

hatch.name: Hatch
hatch.what_you_get: AI-powered text-back and follow-up sequences for home services
hidden_costs: Pricing not transparent -- requires sales call
weaknesses: Template-based responses, not truly intelligent AI
Cannot handle complex or nuanced conversations
Opaque pricing requires sales call
Lock-in contracts reported
Generic templates feel robotic to homeowners
Limited customization per business type
Slow to adapt to new service categories

numa.name: Numa
numa.what_you_get: AI phone answering focused on restaurants and local businesses
weaknesses: Phone-focused, not text-back native
Originally built for restaurants, adapted for home services
AI phone conversations feel robotic
Text capabilities are secondary feature
Not specialized for home services sales
Cannot learn business-specific details deeply
Response quality drops for complex service questions

nothing.name: Doing Nothing
nothing.what_you_get: Missed calls go to voicemail. Maybe you call back tomorrow.
hidden_costs: Average missed call costs home services business $500-$3,000 in lost revenue
78% of customers hire the first business that responds
Weekend and evening calls = highest-value emergency jobs
Reputation damage from slow response times
weaknesses: Not a competitor -- this is the status quo
Every hour without coverage is money left on the table
Voicemail-to-callback takes hours or days
Owner burnout from being on-call 24/7
Cannot scale personal phone answering

price_per_lead.nevermiss: Fixed $297/mo regardless of volume. 100 leads = $2.97 each.

availability.nevermiss: 24/7/365. AI never sleeps, never calls in sick, never takes vacation.
availability.smith_ai: Business hours only. Evenings, weekends, holidays = voicemail.
availability.ruby: Business hours primary. After-hours is add-on with limited coverage.
availability.hatch: AI-based so technically 24/7, but template quality drops off-hours.
availability.numa: AI-based 24/7 for phone, but text-back is secondary.
availability.nothing: Whenever you personally answer. Hope you never sleep.

intelligence.nevermiss: Learns your business. Understands HVAC vs plumbing vs electrical. Adapts.
intelligence.smith_ai: Human follows script. Script is static. No learning over time.
intelligence.ruby: Human follows script. Different human each time = inconsistent.
intelligence.hatch: Template matching. Picks closest template. No real understanding.
intelligence.numa: Basic AI. Built for restaurant orders, adapted for home services.
intelligence.nothing: You know your business best, but you cannot be everywhere.
